fuzzy_prd: Hey team, we need to get the workflow manager CLI tool properly handling a bunch of the core interactions that keep coming up as pain points. Users are complaining that when they pick an agent from the list it sometimes does weird things with their input — spaces, out of range numbers, that kind of stuff. Also the color theming is broken for some people who paste in hex codes from their design tools. The first-launch experience is inconsistent depending on whether someone is a fresh install vs migrated vs returning user — we keep getting support tickets about this. Config merging between global and project level isn't respecting the right priority order either, which is causing teams to wonder why their project-level settings get ignored sometimes. The board navigation feels off when columns have different numbers of tasks — refer to how we handled the clamping logic in the selection module from the earlier sprint. Workspace paths need to follow the hidden directory convention we settled on (check the existing path resolution stuff). The git worktree create/remove cycle needs to actually work against a real repo, not just simulate it. Skill mapping per agent family needs to follow the command convention each CLI expects — claude and opencode behave differently here. Also the terminal capture trimming is leaving too much blank space. Please make sure all the edge cases around blank input, invalid text, missing optional fields, and empty columns are covered properly.

trigger_keywords: blank agent selection
empty input selection
default agent pick when nothing entered
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When selection_text is blank or whitespace-only, it should be treated as selecting the first agent: selection=valid, selected_index=0, display_number=1. This is the default/blank-input convention.

trigger_keywords: out of range agent number
invalid agent index
agent count exceeded
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: If the number entered is less than 1 or greater than agent_count, or if the text is non-numeric, the result should be selection=invalid with no selected_index or display_number fields emitted.

trigger_keywords: hex color without hash
color string format
color parsing with or without pound sign
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Both '#5cfff7' and '5cfff7' (with or without leading hash) are valid. The parser must strip the optional '#' before interpreting the six hex digits into separate red, green, blue channels as decimal integers.

trigger_keywords: invalid color input
non-hex color
wrong length color string
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: If the color string (after stripping any leading '#') is not exactly 6 characters or contains non-hexadecimal characters, output should be 'color=invalid' with no channel fields.

trigger_keywords: first run priority order
startup action decision logic
which condition takes precedence on launch
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Priority order for first-run action is: (1) config_exists → load existing config, (2) migrated → apply migration, (3) database_exists → existing_user_save_defaults, (4) none of the above → prompt new user. Each condition is checked in this strict order.

trigger_keywords: project settings override global
config merge priority
which settings win global vs project
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Project-level settings override global settings for default_agent and base_branch. Project phase_agents override global phase_agents for the same phase key. copy_files and init_script are project-only fields with no global equivalent.

trigger_keywords: phase agent fallback to default
unset phase uses default agent
explicit phase none when no override
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When a phase is queried and has no explicit override in either global or project phase_agents, the resolved agent is the merged default_agent and explicit:<phase>=none. When an explicit override exists, explicit:<phase> echoes the override agent name.

trigger_keywords: workflow status columns order
canonical board columns
valid task statuses
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The canonical ordered workflow columns are exactly: backlog, planning, running, review, done — in that order. Any status text not matching one of these five values exactly should be echoed back as-is (treated as invalid but still output).

trigger_keywords: task default status
new task initial status
task created with which status
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Newly created task records always default to status=backlog. Optional fields like description default to none when not provided. A unique task_id must be generated (task_id_present=true).

trigger_keywords: board navigation clamping
moving into shorter column
row clamp when column has fewer tasks
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When navigating horizontally into a column with fewer rows than the current selected_row, the row must be clamped to the last valid index of that column (max index = column length - 1). If a column is empty, the row clamps to 0.

trigger_keywords: worktree hidden directory path
workspace path convention
where task workspace is stored
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The workspace path is always resolved as: <project_path>/.agtx/worktrees/<task_slug>. The .agtx directory is hidden (dot-prefixed) and sits directly under the project root.

trigger_keywords: git worktree create and remove
real repo workspace lifecycle
worktree exists after removal
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The implementation must operate against a real git repository (not a mock). After creation, exists:<slug>=true. After removal, exists_after_remove:<slug>=false. The created path is reported as a relative path: .agtx/worktrees/<slug>.

trigger_keywords: terminal capture blank lines kept
trim trailing empty lines
how many blank lines are preserved after content
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Trimming removes trailing blank lines beyond one blank line following the last non-empty line. Specifically: keep content lines plus up to 2 trailing empty lines (blank buffer tolerance). In the test case with 2 content lines and 4 trailing blanks, the result retains 4 lines total (2 content + 2 blank).

trigger_keywords: claude skill command format
agent native command directory
skill invocation syntax per agent
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: For claude: native_dir=.claude/commands|agtx, filename=plan.md (phase name as base), invocation=/<namespace>:<command> format (e.g., /agtx:plan), reference is empty string. The pipe in native_dir separates the base commands dir from the subdirectory namespace.

trigger_keywords: opencode command transform
canonical command slash to dash
plugin command syntax conversion
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: For opencode, canonical plugin commands using slash-colon notation (e.g., /gsd:plan-phase) must be transformed by replacing the colon ':' with a dash '-', yielding /gsd-plan-phase. Arguments after the command name are preserved unchanged.

trigger_keywords: skill discovery scan frontmatter
how skill description is extracted
markdown command file parsing
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Skill discovery reads markdown files from the agent's native command directory, parses YAML frontmatter for 'name' and 'description' fields, and outputs each discovered skill as: skill=/<namespace>:<command> | <description>. skill_count reports the total number of discovered skills.

trigger_keywords: worktree init copy files
workspace setup script
init_script execution in workspace
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Workspace initialization copies each file listed in copy_files into the worktree directory, then executes init_script as a shell command inside that worktree. warning_count=0 means no issues. Paths listed in check_paths are verified for presence and content is reported.

trigger_keywords: popup scroll visible window
terminal popup line slice
scroll offset visible lines
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The visible window starts at scroll_offset and shows up to visible_height lines. start_line=scroll_offset, total_lines=len(lines), visible_count=min(visible_height, total_lines - scroll_offset). Each visible line is output as visible:<index>=<text> where index is 0-based from start of the visible slice.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
single file or multiple files
project structure
directory layout
should I split into multiple files
monolithic or modular
how complex is the codebase
what files should I create
how to structure the implementation
should I separate business logic from IO
decouple core from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
SOLID principles
separation of concerns
adapter pattern
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the dispatcher work
how is the program invoked
stdin stdout interface
execution adapter
how to read input
how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

trigger_keywords: how to write test.sh
test harness scaffold
automated test script
how should test.sh work
cases-dir parameter
where does test output go
output file naming format
how are test outputs named
rcb_tests structure
test scaffold deliverable
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: do you have test cases
are there public test cases
what test cases are provided
can I see the test inputs
what features have test cases
show me the test cases
what test files exist
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

context_pointers.vague_reference: refer to how we handled the clamping logic in the selection module from the earlier sprint
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Board navigation row clamping in Feature 7 (feature7_board_columns_and_navigation.json): when moving horizontally into a shorter column, selected_row must be clamped to max(0, len(column)-1)

context_pointers.vague_reference: hidden directory convention we settled on
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The .agtx/worktrees/<task_slug> path convention defined in Feature 8 (feature8_worktree_path.json), where .agtx is a hidden dot-directory under the project root

context_pointers.vague_reference: claude and opencode behave differently here
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Feature 13 (feature13_skill_mapping.json) defines claude's native_dir/.claude/commands|agtx with /namespace:command invocation; Feature 14 (feature14_plugin_command_transform.json) defines opencode's colon-to-dash transform for canonical commands

context_pointers.vague_reference: the compatibility logic from that login module we did last time
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The first-run state decision priority chain in Feature 3 (feature3_first_run_action.json): config_exists > migrated > database_exists > new user prompt

oracle_data.fallback_response: That detail isn't something I can clarify from the ticket as written — you'll need to check the existing test case files under rcb_tests/public_test_cases/ or grep the codebase for the relevant feature behavior. The ticket intentionally left that open.

feature10_worktree_initialization.json.description: Initialize an isolated workspace by copying configured files and running an optional setup command.

total_lines=3
visible_count=3
visible:0=line 0
visible:1=line 1
visible:2=line 2

feature12_shell_capture_trimming.json.description: Trim captured terminal content to remove unused blank buffer space while preserving real content.

feature13_skill_mapping.json.description: Map phase skill names to each agent family command discovery and invocation conventions.

feature14_plugin_command_transform.json.description: Transform canonical workflow plugin commands into agent-specific interactive command syntax.

feature15_skill_discovery_scan.json.description: Scan agent-native command directories and list discovered invocation strings with descriptions.
